chosen:
def lexicographic_next(text):
    a=list(text);i=len(a)-2
    while i>=0 and a[i]>=a[i+1]:i-=1
    if i<0:return None
    j=len(a)-1
    while a[j]<=a[i]:j-=1
    a[i],a[j]=a[j],a[i];a[i+1:]=reversed(a[i+1:])
    return "".join(a)

prompt:

def next_permutation(items):

Task: mutate list to its next lexicographic permutation and return True, or reset ascending and return False. Mutate the exact list; duplicates are allowed.
Return only Python code. Do not include Markdown fences or explanations.
chosen:
def next_permutation(items):
    i=len(items)-2
    while i>=0 and items[i]>=items[i+1]: i-=1
    if i<0: items.reverse(); return False
    j=len(items)-1
    while items[j]<=items[i]: j-=1
    items[i],items[j]=items[j],items[i]; items[i+1:]=reversed(items[i+1:])
    return True
